title: Implement identifier-normalization service (genes/variants/drugs/diseases/pathways)
context: Multi-source integration requires a shared identifier-normalization service that resolves entity identifiers from different sources to canonical CURIEs. The policy is defined in oncogene-kg-id-001; this task implements it. Gene/variant/drug/disease/pathway identifiers from different sources must resolve to HGNC/VRS/ChEMBL/Mondo/Reactome canonical CURIEs via Bioregistry, with source-native IDs retained as aliases. Variant merges require human review. Builds on the CIViC data slice (oncogene-kg-data-civic-001).
objective: Implement the identifier-normalization service that resolves each entity class to its canonical CURIE using Bioregistry, retains source-native aliases, precision-tunes variant merges with human review, and meets the >=99% entity resolution and >=95% VRS-keyed variant targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolves gene entities to HGNC/Ensembl canonical CURIEs via Bioregistry; >=99% of a sampled set resolve to a live record.
Resolves variant entities to GA4GH VRS canonical identifiers; >=95% of variant nodes carry a canonical VRS identifier.
Resolves drug entities to ChEMBL/RxNorm, disease entities to Mondo/DOID, pathway entities to Reactome canonical CURIEs.
Source-native IDs are retained as aliases on every resolved entity, not discarded.
Variant merges require human review; no silent auto-merge is implemented.
Service integrates into the ETL pipeline and passes CI.
output: etl/normalize/ directory containing the identifier-normalization service code, committed via PR with CI green.
